vulnerablePopulations: Northern EU countries with limited sunlight and vitamin D deficiency
Individuals with family history or low BMI
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: WHO DXA T-score ≤ -2.5, FRAX tool widely used

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: opportunistic screening, DXA widely available in most countries

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: ESCEO/IOF guidelines, FRAX tool for fracture risk assessment
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Bisphosphonates (alendronate, risedronate), denosumab, teriparatide for severe cases; calcium + vitamin D supplementation
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Aging demographics increasing burden, treatment gaps despite availability, high fracture-related healthcare costs, vitamin D deficiency in northern countries
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Comprehensive ESCEO guidelines, wide DXA access, fracture liaison services in many countries, high awareness, high treatment availability
